title: Open work permit for vulnerable workers: Who can apply - Canada.ca
content:
## To exit this page quickly:
If your abuser monitors your computer, delete your browsing history .
- are in Canada You can’t apply at a port of entry.
- You can’t apply at a port of entry.
- are being abused or at risk of being abused in relation to your job in Canada, and
- have a valid (not expired) employer-specific work permit with your employer’s name on it, or
- applied to extend your work permit before it expired and you’re still waiting on a decision
Abuse can be any behaviour that scares, controls or isolates you. It can be physical, sexual, financial, psychological or reprisal.
Examples of abuse that you should report in your application include:
- forcing you to work in a way that’s unsafe or puts your health at risk
- unsafe or unsanitary living conditions in employer-provided housing
- sexual touching that you did not agree to
- making unwanted sexual comments to you
- controlling where you can go
- stopping you from seeing friends or co-workers
- taking some or all of the money you are paid
- threats, insults and intimidation
- forcing you to commit fraud
- a third party charging you fees for a job or making promises that aren’t real
- any disciplinary measure, demotion, dismissal or threat to take any of these measures because you’ve reported non-compliance with working conditions or cooperated with an employer inspection
